title: Eustephia brevituba
taxonomy: Eustephia brevituba is classified within the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. This family is a group of flowering plants that include irises, gladiolus, and crocuses. Eustephia brevituba is native to the Andes mountains in South America and is a part of the subfamily Iridoideae. The classification of Eustephia brevituba is further divided into the genus Eustephia, which comprises a small number of species. The specific classification of Eustephia brevituba is still being researched and may be subject to change.
benefits: Eustephia brevituba, a plant from the Fabaceae family, has been found to have various health benefits, although more research is needed to fully understand its properties.
